steps.title: Connect volume to pleasure
steps.content: Musicians and nonmusicians could detect sounds equally well, but musicians chose higher volumes as most pleasurable. The conclusion must explain this as a difference in hedonic response to volume, not as a difference in basic hearing ability.

steps.title: Reject contradicted claims
steps.content: Choice A says earlier brain research may be overstated, but the study found a real preference difference. Choice C shifts the pleasure response away from volume, though volume was the tested variable. Choice D contradicts the equal sound-detection finding. The remaining idea explains why musicians need higher volume for the same pleasure response. The answer is B.
